description: Enhance the evolution loop with quieter default output, optional idea-file proposing, versioned engine artifacts in chess-engine/versions, and more flexible inconclusive-decision handling.

userStories.title: Suppress raw OpenClaude output by default
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the evolution loop to suppress the OpenClaude banner and raw phase output so the terminal stays readable during unattended runs.
acceptanceCriteria: The orchestration script does not print the OpenClaude banner during propose, implement, benchmark, or decide phases
The orchestration script does not stream raw skill output to the terminal by default
The default output behavior matches scripts/ralph/ralph.sh with concise terminal progress and captured underlying tool output
Each phase still captures stdout/stderr for later inspection in session artifacts or logs
The final per-phase outcome remains available in iteration artifacts
bash -n scripts/evolution-loop.sh passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Set the default Claude CLI binary to openclaude and changed per-phase execution to capture stdout/stderr into iteration phase log artifacts instead of streaming raw CLI output to the terminal. The loop now records phase log paths in iteration.json while keeping concise terminal summaries only.

userStories.title: Show current and remaining phases
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the loop to show the current phase and the remaining phases for the active iteration so I can tell whether the run is progressing or stuck.
acceptanceCriteria: At the start of each iteration, the script prints the ordered phase list for that iteration
Before each phase begins, the script prints the current phase name
Before each phase begins, the script prints which phases remain in the current iteration
Output is concise and does not include raw model banner text
bash -n scripts/evolution-loop.sh passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added a fixed iteration phase sequence and concise progress helpers so each iteration prints the full phase order once, then prints the current phase and remaining phases before propose, implement, validate, benchmark, and decide. Raw model output remains suppressed behind the phase logs introduced in US-001.

userStories.title: Accept optional ideas file
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to pass a file of possible engine ideas so the propose phase can prefer curated ideas generated by me or other agents.
acceptanceCriteria: The script accepts a new optional argument for an ideas file path
If the ideas file is omitted, the propose phase behaves exactly as it does today
If the ideas file exists but is empty, the propose phase ignores it and behaves like no file was provided
The supported format is Markdown checklist entries using '- [ ] idea text'
The propose phase can read pending ideas from that file during hypothesis selection
bash -n scripts/evolution-loop.sh passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Track hypothesis source and mark used ideas
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want each iteration to record whether the hypothesis came from the user ideas file or the model itself so versioning and audit behavior can follow the correct policy.
acceptanceCriteria: iteration.json stores the proposal source as either 'user_ideas_file' or 'self_proposed'
hypothesis.md states the source of the selected idea
The implementation and decision phases can read the proposal source from iteration.json
After an idea from the checklist file is selected and tested, the system marks that checklist entry as used
bash -n scripts/evolution-loop.sh passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bump candidate UCI version
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the implementation phase to bump the engine version shown in the UCI name so each candidate can be distinguished during benchmarking.
acceptanceCriteria: The implement phase updates the engine version string exposed in the UCI name for the candidate build
If the hypothesis source is self_proposed, the candidate version increments the minor version from the current baseline, for example v0.1 to v0.2
If the hypothesis source is user_ideas_file, the candidate version increments the major version from the current baseline, for example v0.1 to v1.0
The candidate version is recorded in iteration.json and implementation.md
Candidate and baseline UCI names are distinguishable during benchmark runs
The implementation phase produces a candidate binary named wiggum-engine for later persistence into version artifacts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Resolve baseline from stored version artifacts
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the loop to use stored engine versions from chess-engine/versions instead of git refs so baseline selection is explicit and reproducible from release artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: The session accepts a baseline version label that resolves to chess-engine/versions/<tag>
The baseline version directory is required to contain a compiled engine binary named wiggum-engine
The loop fails fast with a clear error if the requested baseline version directory is missing or if wiggum-engine is absent
session.env records the selected baseline version path instead of a git ref
iteration.json records the resolved baseline version path used for that iteration
The benchmark phase can identify the baseline engine binary from the stored version directory
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist accepted engine binaries as new versions
userStories.description: As an operator, I want accepted candidates to be saved as versioned engine artifacts so future iterations and external benchmarking use the promoted binary directly.
acceptanceCriteria: When an iteration is accepted, the script creates a new directory chess-engine/versions/<new-version>
The accepted candidate compiled engine binary is copied into that version directory with the filename wiggum-engine
The version directory does not require preserving the Cargo target layout
The stored binary path is recorded in session metadata for future iterations
The script does not depend on a git commit ref to resolve the next baseline
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update active baseline after promotion
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the loop to update the active baseline after a candidate is accepted so the next iteration evolves from the latest promoted model.
acceptanceCriteria: After an accepted decision, the session metadata updates the active baseline version to the new promoted version
After an accepted decision, the next iteration uses the stored binary from the new version directory
After a rejected, failed, or inconclusive decision without promotion, the active baseline remains unchanged
The final session summary lists accepted baseline promotions in order
bash -n scripts/evolution-loop.sh passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run extra Stockfish comparison for inconclusive results
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the decision flow to trigger an extra comparison against Stockfish when candidate-vs-baseline results are inconclusive so promotion decisions use more context than a single neutral match.
acceptanceCriteria: If the baseline-vs-candidate benchmark result is inconclusive, the decision flow can request or run an additional Stockfish comparison
The comparison uses the prior stored baseline benchmark result against Stockfish when available
The candidate Stockfish result is recorded in benchmark.md or a linked decision artifact
The decision artifact explicitly states whether the Stockfish comparison changed the recommendation
If the Stockfish comparison cannot run, the iteration records that limitation instead of silently ignoring it
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Allow positive-signal promotion after inconclusive evidence
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the decision phase to promote an inconclusive candidate when there is still a positive signal after extra analysis so potentially stronger engines are not discarded too aggressively.
acceptanceCriteria: If the direct candidate-vs-baseline result is inconclusive, the decision logic performs the additional Stockfish comparison before making the final call
If the Stockfish comparison is also inconclusive, the decision logic explicitly evaluates whether there is any positive signal suggesting likely improvement
If no positive signal exists, the candidate is not promoted
If a positive signal exists and the candidate is promoted, decision.md explains why promotion was allowed despite inconclusive match evidence
iteration.json records the exact evidence used for the promotion decision
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add optional verbose mode
userStories.description: As an operator, I want an optional verbose mode so I can debug suppressed skill output when the loop appears stalled or unclear.
acceptanceCriteria: The script accepts a verbose flag that enables live skill output for debugging
The default mode remains the quieter Ralph-like output mode
Verbose mode does not change iteration artifacts or decision behavior
bash -n scripts/evolution-loop.sh passes
Typecheck passes
